steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts how two films were received initially with how they are regarded now. The 2018 evidence pins down the current view: critics called the films “galvanizing” and “exuberant” — both strongly positive. The blank must match that positive tone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Favorably’: means with approval or positive regard — matches “galvanizing” and “exuberant” exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘skeptically’: means with doubt or distrust; contradicts the enthusiastic praise. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘neutrally’: means without strong opinion; the critics’ language is clearly enthusiastic, not neutral. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘strangely’: means in an odd or unusual way; nothing in the evidence suggests the current regard is strange. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
